title: 10 best herbal creams for hyperacidity

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with hyperacidity because it contains glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to increase the production of mucus in the stomach, which can help neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from heartburn and acid reflux symptoms. remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which provides anti-inflammatory and antacid properties.

remedy_recipe: Mix 20 grams of finely ground glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely.
Add 5 grams of sweet almond oil and 2 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Stir in 10 grams of distilled water and 2 grams of carbopol 980 at room temperature for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Weigh and crush 200 grams of aloe vera gel to release its natural soothing properties.
Combine 50 grams of coconut oil with 30 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 20 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the melted mixture.
Blend the mixture thoroughly and pour it into a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely before applying to skin as a cream.

Mix the powder with 100 grams of shea butter and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat for another 5 minutes stirring constantly.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of camellia sinensis leaves and dry them in a dehydrator at 45°c for 12 hours.
Steep 200g of dried camellia sinensis leaves in 1000ml of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the camellia sinensis infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Combine the camellia sinensis infusion with the beeswax and coconut oil mixture and whip until thickened.

Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to a year.

remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum seeds and dry them properly using shade drying method within 24 hours.
Grind the dried silybum marianum seeds into fine powder using coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 250ml distilled water with 20g of powdered silybum marianum extract in a heatproof glass bowl for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture gently over low flame stirring occasionally until it reaches 60-70°c within 15-20 minutes.
Allow the cooled mixture to infuse for 2 hours and then whip with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax at room temperature for 10 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hyperacidity are topical remedies that help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with excessive stomach acid production. These creams typically contain a combination of soothing herbs that work to neutralize acid, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. The best herbal creams for hyperacidity include Zingiber officinale, Aloe vera, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Cinchona officinalis, Tamarindus indica, Mentha x piperita, Centella asiatica, Aloe barbadensis, and Foeniculum vulgare. If left untreated, hyperacidity can lead to digestive issues, sleep disturbances, and anxiety, ultimately impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperacidity that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, acne, rosacea, and minor burns. Herbal creams can soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. For example, aloe vera is often used to treat minor burns and sunburns, while tea tree oil is commonly used to combat acne and rosacea due to its antimicrobial properties.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with hyperacidity is Aloe Vera and Ghee enriched Neem cream. Aloe Vera helps soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The antibacterial properties of Neem cream aid in balancing gut bacteria, preventing the growth of acid-producing bacteria. Together, they form a potent blend to alleviate hyperacidity symptoms, promote healing, and restore digestive balance. This natural combination can be applied topically to provide quick relief and promote long-term healing.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 5g of dried zingiber officinale root in 50ml of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container discard solids.
Combine 15ml of the infused oil with 25g of beeswax and 5ml of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160-180f for 10-15 minutes or until melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it solidify at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with hyperacidity because of its soothing and cooling properties. remedy_recipe: Collect 200g of aloe vera gel from 2 mature leaves and store it in an airtight container.
Combine 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler at 120°f.
Add 20g of shea butter and 10g of jojoba oil to the melted beeswax mixture in 5 minutes.
Gradually add the aloe vera gel to the melted mixture while stirring continuously for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely in 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and store it in a clean container for later use.
Combine 10 grams of distilled water with 10 grams of vegetable glycerin in a small saucepan.
Add 20 grams of sweet almond oil and 5 grams of beeswax to the saucepan and heat gently.
Stir in the glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and remove the saucepan from the heat source immediately.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for about 30 minutes before transferring it to a glass jar.

Grind 1 part of dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 250g of tamarindus indica fruits in 1l of carrier oil for 4 weeks in a cool dark place.

Mix 150g of beeswax with 30g of candelilla wax in a double boiler at 180-190°f for 10 minutes.

Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard solids.

remedy_constituents: Anethole: It helps reduce hyperacidity by inhibiting the production of stomach acid and soothing the gastrointestinal tract.
Ferulic acid: It acts as an antioxidant, reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes from acid damage, thereby alleviating hyperacidity symptoms.
Trans-anethol: It possesses carminative properties, helping to expel gas and reduce bloating, which can contribute to hyperacidity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, they help neutralize stomach acid and soothe digestive issues.
Seeds: Containing a compound called anethole, they have a natural carminative effect, relieving bloating and indigestion.
Fruits: Also known as seeds, they are often used in herbal remedies to treat hyperacidity due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves from a well-maintained organic garden.
Chop the harvested foeniculum vulgare into small pieces using a sharp knife within 15 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of chopped foeniculum vulgare with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 2 hours and 30 minutes to allow the oil to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and bottle the resulting foeniculum vulgare cream within 30 minutes.
